blockingIssues.description: The rerun fixes the mocked REST-to-Realtime websocket-hop gap for VAL-CROSS-009, but the paired mock/live equivalence proof still fabricates realtime `event_ordering` from the surface name instead of deriving it from observed live report data, so VAL-CROSS-004 remains unproven.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit Exec Mode guidance for satisfying the required brainstorming step without an approval loop, so workers can comply procedurally while still executing publish-facing doc validation autonomously.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `publish-ready-fix-markdown-snippet-and-command-validation` passed on substance but still recorded a procedural deviation because `sdk-publish-worker` requires brainstorming in a mode with no user approval loop.

rejectedObservations.observation: The `sdk-surface-worker` skill should explicitly mention tiny combined REST+websocket local harnesses for proof-only cross-surface fixes.
rejectedObservations.reason: Overlaps the prior round's broader sdk-surface-worker guidance recommendation about proof-only cross-surface helper guidance, so I did not add a duplicate recommendation.
